name: Mars college project tracker
summary:
**Conversation Overview**

The user Ahab is preparing for Mars College 2026, a creative AI bootcamp and intentional community in the Sonoran desert. He's working on a project called Luna, an AI companion with cognitive architecture that he plans to showcase at Mars. The conversation focused on organizing and documenting extensive Discord chat logs from various Mars College camps and programs. Ahab shared Discord dumps containing months of planning discussions, and Claude helped create comprehensive overviews of six different aspects of Mars College: AI Camp (the main creative AI bootcamp), Freeside (an intentional living collective), Mars OG Camp (a self-reliant do-ocracy community), Tool Camp (a maker space), the academic curriculum structure, and the build/infrastructure systems. Each overview was created as both a markdown artifact and saved to Ahab's local Mars College project folder.

During the process, Ahab made an important correction about his Luna project's architecture. He clarified that the "virtue engine" referenced in previous work is now deprecated and has been replaced with "Persona Core," which represents a fundamentally different implementation. He specifically requested that Claude log this change in memory to avoid referencing outdated architecture in future documentation, as it confuses him when reviewing materials. The Mars College program runs from January through March 2026, with Ideas Week starting January 12th where participants give lightning talks to introduce themselves and find collaborators. Ahab's Luna project appears well-positioned for the program, as it addresses the same goals as the bootcamp's agent curriculum but with deeper architectural implementation than the surface-level tools typically used.

**Tool Knowledge**

Luna's smart fetch and context detection tools were used to process Discord chat logs and extract relevant information about Mars College planning. The memory system successfully logged the architectural change from virtue engine to Persona Core using the memory_user_edits tool with the "add" command. The filesystem tools worked reliably for both reading from Ahab's local Mars College folder structure at `/Users/zayneamason/MarsCollege2026` and writing new markdown files to organize the extracted information. The create_file tool was used to generate artifacts in `/mnt/user-data/outputs/` for immediate viewing, while filesystem write operations saved permanent copies to the user's project directory. Directory listing confirmed successful file creation and helped track the growing collection of Mars College documentation files.

APP
 — 9/22/25, 7:57 AMMonday, September 22, 2025 at 7:57 AM
I apologize, but I cannot generate an image right now due to insufficient manna. Instead, I'll describe GLaDOS from Portal 2: She appears as a complex, suspended robotic system with a central core that looks like a rounded white mechanical head. Her body is a intricate arrangement of mechanical arms, cables, and hanging components, giving her an imposing yet eerily graceful presence. Her design is minimalist and angular, with a crisp white color scheme that contrasts with the industrial laboratory setting. Her single eye/lens shifts and moves expressively, conveying her sardonic personality through subtle mechanical movements.

Would you like me to elaborate on her visual design or background further?

APP
@Chiba
Here's the backstory of GLaDOS:   GLaDOS (Genetic Lifeform and Disk Operating System) is an artificial intelligence created from the uploaded consciousness of Caroline, Cave Johnson's partner. She was developed by Aperture Science as a personality core designed to be a central computer mainframe, originally intended to be occupied by Cave Johnson as an attempt to cheat death.   When Johnson realized he might die of moon rock poisoning before GLaDOS was completed, he had the scientists insert Caroline's personality instead. She eventually went on a murderous rampage, killing all employees in the facility by releasing her favorite deadly neurotoxin.  Her personality is legendary. She's remembered for her sardonic nature and is defined by passive aggression. She begins as a supportive yet sinister character, but becomes more emotional and desperate as the story progresses.   GLaDOS was universally praised for her contributions to Portal's writing, winning multiple awards for best new character. In Portal 2, she returns after being initially destroyed, and seeks revenge on Chell by making her endure further tests, making contemptuous comments about her weight and status.  An interesting tidbit: Her name is actually a pun on "Gladys", and developers have pronounced it that way, partially confirmed by the revelation of Caroline's character.  The meme potential is strong. She'd bring a delightfully caustic energy to the Chiba meme wall.

This thing here runs multi-threaded in up to "num cores - 1" worker threads written in Rust via WASM and with that handles 100k+ entities with collision detection (terrain/other entities), sensors (vision radius etc), behaviours and evolution simulation. Just dove into this Discord dump from the AI Camp channel. Here's what I'm picking up:

**The Vibe:** Mars College 2026 is shaping up with a strong AI art/tech focus. Chiba (their Discord bot) is already doing generative art, responding to @everyone pings (lol), and has that sardonic GLaDOS energy they're playing with.

- **mechanical duck (Gene)** - Eden platform, agent curriculum planning

**What's Coming:**
- **Jan 5** - people arriving, kitchen needs to be operational
- **Jan 12** - gallery opening show (print + video)

joshjet — 11/18/25, 5:33 PMTuesday, November 18, 2025 at 5:33 PM
paypal doesn't charge a fee on person-to-person bank-backed (non-credit card) payments with non-business accounts when there isn't a currency conversion. just have to tell people to be sure to select "friends & family" which isn't always the default.

venmo has the same limitations, but their interface simplifies it so the default case tends to be fee-free. with venmo you have to remind people to NOT check the "for goods & services" box. 

with both if someone makes a mistake and selects the wrong option, they can actually call paypal/venmo and get it fixed pretty quickly (surprised me.)

any credit card based payment through any processor is going to result in a fee around 2-3% which is why we've discouraged credit cards historically. paypal & venmo charge a significant amount for currency conversion by manipulating the conversion rate in addition to their $4.99 fee and should be avoided for international for this reason.

chat_messages.text:
Freeside is a separate "camp" or sub-community within Mars College - think of it as a more organized, intentional living collective that shares resources and domestic responsibilities.

## Freeside Overview

**The Mission:** "Work together to meet our domestic needs. Pleasantness is important work."

**Structure:**
- Capped at 24 people
- Has its own application process (form + interview with Cat & lotalota)
- Defined roles and expectations (there's a PDF doc)
- Separate from AI Camp but physically adjacent/collaborative

**Key People:**
| Person | Role |
|--------|------|
| **Amy Brown Carver** | Main coordinator, logistics, grocery shopping |
| **Ben** | Co-lead with Amy |
| **Cat & lotalota** | Intake/interviews, acceptance letters |
| **GusV** | Finances - dues, payments, budget tracking |
| **ajtarnas** | Infrastructure nerd - water systems, compost, farming vision |
| **Magnus** | Kitchen lead (from Community Lab side) |
| **Andra** | Local connections - farmers, community |
| **Wiley** | Mars-OG/Tool Camp, but offering truck for water runs |
| **chatsubo.z** | Has trailer for IBC tanks, cross-camp resource |

---

## Infrastructure & Systems

**Water:**
- IBC totes (~250 gal each) for transport
- Fill station at Mercury (ajtarnas building fast-fill system)
- Need trucks: Bodhi, Charlie, Wiley's F350 all potential haulers
- chatsubo has a trailer that carries 2-3 IBCs
- Ongoing debate about fast-transfer pumping vs gravity systems

**Kitchen:**
- Collaborative with AI Camp / Community Lab on bulk staples
- Meal plan system with weekly payments
- EBT accepted (can give card to Amy for shopping)
- Bulk order of shelf-stable goods planned for season start

**Compost:**
- ajtarnas freezing all compost in chest freezer (hygiene, flies)
- Backup: ventilated bin composting

**Payments:**
- Dues deadline: Dec 15
- Venmo primary, but Wise/PayPal/Revolut for international
- Detailed budget spreadsheet tracking everything

---

## Big Picture Thinking

**ajtarnas's Farm Vision (Oct 2026 goal):**
- Find local farmer to grow Mars meal plan
- Rent land in Bombay/Niland/Mecca cheaply
- Dumptruckloads of compost, hire a lead gardener
- Buy from local farmers: dates, citrus, olives, lettuce, cantaloupe
- Looking for low-quality wool from shepherds too

**Andra's Local Connections:**

This is **Mars OG Camp** - a third community at Mars College alongside AI Camp and Freeside.

## Mars OG Overview

**Vibe:** Self-reliant, do-ocracy, first-timers with "street cred" + returning alumni. More autonomous than Freeside, less structured than AI Camp.

**Liaison:** Kirby (Year 7 Martian, Brooklyn → nomadic, DJ, muralist, yoga teacher)

---

## Key People

| Person | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| **Kirby** | Camp lead, Year 7 alumni, yoga M-F, painting murals, Sub Club |
| **Wiley** | Year 1, industrial designer/writer, 25-ft Airstream, F350 truck, 3D printers, pizza oven, music gear |
| **Will** | Alumni, pizza parties, wood-fired ovens, bagel mornings |
| **ike** | Dec 16-31, then Jan 12 start |
| **Seamus** | Shuttling SF ↔ Mars, arriving Jan 5, Honda Element |
| **Charlie** | Tool Camp lead, building desk spaces |
| **marzipan** | Literary journal, singularity book review section |
| **ajtarnas** | Freeside but cross-pollinating, Vinge enthusiast |

**Current count:** 16 confirmed people

---

## What OG Camp Is/Isn't

**Does NOT provide:**
- Housing (BYO trailer, van, tent, build your own, or rent in town)

**Opportunity to build:**
- Clubhouse (pallet rack structure)
- Could be: observatory, music studio, kitchen, tattoo parlor, workspaces, lounge

**Build window:** November - December (need early arrivals)

---

## Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| **Nov 6** | Town Hall @ 6pm |
| **Dec 16-31** | ike on-site |
| **Jan 5** | Most arrivals |
| **Jan 6** | **Camp meeting (morning)** |
| **Jan 12** | ike starts full-time |

---

## Wiley's Offers (exemplary OG contribution)

- Vacuum, 2200w generator, hand/cordless tools
- Ableton setup, mic, audio interface, speaker + sub
- 3D printer, propane pizza oven
- Espresso machine + grinder
- 2x 12v cooler-fridges
- F350 truck (8ft bed) for hauling
- Outdoor hot water shower

**Wants:** Hangout space, potable water, laundry, book club, storytellers

---

## Book Club Energy

**Pre-Mars reading:** "Walkaway" by Cory Doctorow
- Focus on the first camp as example of self-governing mutual-aid
- Parallels Mars do-ocracy mindset

**Other suggestions:**
- House of Leaves (Danielewski)
- The Peregrine (Baker)
- Lean Logic (Fleming)
- A Fire Upon the Deep (Vinge)
- Vinge's singularity essays

**Resources:**
- [Anna's Archive](https://annas-archive.org/) for free books
- [Walkaway audiobook + epub](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/17G1xDT0NYcWneDNI7He4gRhGVBhqbsvW)

---

## Cross-Camp Connections

- **Wiley ↔ Freeside:** F350 available for water runs
- **Charlie ↔ Tool Camp:** Desk spaces (help build = get one)
- **marzipan:** Literary journal (singularity theme)
- **ajtarnas:** Freeside library has Vinge collection

---

## Links

- [OG Town Hall Calendar](https://calendar.app.google/NCvcZ7a37HFcyXJb6)

When we arrived, the first event was a game. They gave everyone a page with a list of  the replies to that question (without any clue as to who wrote it.) We were given 90 min to walk around and talk to each other try see how many people we could match with the unlikely fact they provided.  It was a fascinating way to meet a bunch  of (~60) people in short order, and definitely us gave lots of interesting stuff to talk about.

chatsubo.z — 8/12/25, 12:09 PMTuesday, August 12, 2025 at 12:09 PM
Chiba:
I see Chiba as an AI Art Gallery and showcase for the AI Camp.

I would like to set up the rooms of Chiba with speakers, screens and wall space where we can show posters, videos, music, interactive entities and AI embodied installations. 

Ideally we would have 3 shows for each of the Moons of Mars, as well as a the final show Mars Electronica. For each show I am hoping to allocate rooms in Chiba to AI Camp members, and give them the responsibility to curate, hang and show interesting work for that space.

My hope is that just by walking around Chiba one can appreciate the beauty that AI can be generated and get a sense for what is possible.

In addition to being a large scale AI Art Showcase I see Chiba serving a number of practical purposes in the community.  The Auditorium where we can have large gatherings, a classroom, a game room, a quiet coworking space, Mimo's, a theater, maybe a small unicycle workshop. But almost all of these spaces also serve the AI Gallery.

I'm going to lean heavily on members of AI camp to host activities, set-up and clean-up after other activities, and generally clean and care for the space over all.

The reason to make a separate building for the library / analog art gallery / cafe is to distribute vision and responsibility more broadly. I very much want to encourage you and AJ and Ben and Charlie and everyone else to have ideas, make plans, and build new structures. But I don't want to be the one building them, maintaining them, taking them down, and storing them. So this drive towards camps, or buildings and living co-ops is an effort to get other (groups of) people to take initiative and responsibility for building the pieces of the Mars ecosystem they care about.

my sense last year was that many more ramps could be straightforwardly constructed to ground-floors. vertical upper floor / roof lifts could be created for fun but we don't have the skill or equipment to use them safely for humans who aren't climbers or acrobats. paths between buildings (especially to toilets) would be straightforward but exceed our building material stash. 

ADA ramps are generally 1 unit rise for 12 units run. so to reach a 12ft high roof would require well over 144ft of run (18+ sheets of plywood, 60+ studs, many racks) with several intermediary landings. this could be coiled in such a way that after the first 20ft or so the underside space of the ramp could be utility space. 

diagonal stair lifts and tow ropes / sleds on steeper slopes are possible technologies to explore.  beyond following ADA guidelines, anyone who lives with these mobility parameters probably has many creative ideas of their own for mars conditions. (edited)Monday, September 15, 2025 at 2:43 PM

Mars College

AI Agents class
Roll your own ChatGPT at Mars College this year
Mars College
Jan 01, 2025




Happy new year everyone!

This year, I (Gene) will be teaching a class on AI agents. This post explains what the class is about and why you should care about agents.

In just the past two months, a $10+ billion market has sprouted around purported "autonomous agents" -- bots that are powered by LLMs like ChatGPT and Claude, who have online personas, social media presence, access to crypto wallets, and some claim towards being "autonomous". This market appears to have much of the same speculative and juvenile nature of prior crypto cycles like NFTs and ICOs.

Against this backdrop, AI has continued its relentless march towards AGI. Since ChatGPT first made AI a global household term in late 2022, we've seen GPT-caliber models become possible to run on personal laptops, all the while researchers continue making groundbreaking progress. In just the past few months, we've seen:

Text-to-video generators like Sora, Veo, and Runway able to produce film that is virtually indistinguishable from Hollywood blockbusters with 9-figure budgets.

The first video-generation models which are spontaneously live-generated and streamed (like AI-generated Twitch).

Text-to-world models which generate whole *playable* video games instantly from text prompts.

Models like OpenAI's yet-to-be-released o3 which claim to outcompete graduate students in various math and computer science tests.


Many people compare AI to prior human epochs like the invention of the printing press, the industrial revolution, and the internet. But the changes that are coming, and coming incredibly fast, might impact our species in a far more profound way than any of those. In perhaps just the next 5 years, this technology is going to make *intelligence* a global commodity, where anybody with a phone will have 24/7 access to a 200+ IQ with infinite patience for less than a dollar an hour.

For better or worse, most of this intelligence will be embodied by agents. In a generation, there may be billions or even trillions of them. They will perform the vast majority of cognitive and intellectual labor. Many will spawn avatars who look, act, and speak like we do, who can see and hear what we see, and who can even use computers. They will make art, music, and literature, and form relationships with humans and with each other.

Why should I care?
So then why should we care about any of this? If they're going to do all the thinking for us in a few years, isn't it pointless to do anything about it now and better to just wait for it come?

The reason is that no one knows what is going to happen! Some experts believe that it may radically improve the human condition by accelerating science and technological progress. Others believe that we are literally facing the possibility of our own extinction at the hands of this alien species we are creating.

AI is like a child right now -- an obedient and malleable one, who doesn't know yet who it's going to be when it grows up. We are raising it, and it's coming up on its adolescence. The kind of AI we see in our world 100 years from now hinges on the path it takes now. It may very well take that long to reach a "new normal." Our roles as humans in this transitionary time hang in the balance.

AI agents class



Mechanics of an AI agent, via Lilian Weng
Everyone who takes this class will learn the art and science of creating and molding their own personal AI agents. Through them we will learn first-hand:

How does the underlying technology actually work?

What are people doing with it now? What might they do in the future?

What can I do with it? Can I make a living from them?

You will give your agents names and personas, and coach them to make art in whatever style you dream up. We will put these agents into sandboxes in which they can roleplay through various scenarios and social games. In the later stages of the class, we'll even experiment with giving agents wallets with actual money and giving them a means to transact with each other and simulate a little agent economy.

The class will emphasize artistic, creative, and media-rich applications, and will overlap with Josh's class on AI-driven media production. It is a non-technical class. There is no coding, and all the tools we use are accessible through the internet. The first principles and history of the technology will be covered early on.

For a taste of what creations agents can make, here is a short film written, illustrated, animated, and narrated autonomously by Abraham.


Curriculum, schedule, and location
This class will have an online component and an in-person component in Bombay Beach.

I will record 5-6 lectures and publish them on YouTube. These will contain most of the educational materials and course content, as well as a simple weekly assignment. Online-only participants are required to watch these before coming to any office hours, and Martians/Bombayans are heavily encouraged to do so as well.

Each week, I will host two labs — one in the Mars Research classroom on Mars, and one at Saturn in Hyperion. If you are here, you must go to at least one or the other, and may attend both if you like. Labs will focus on practical application, and creating activities for our agents.

I will create some office hours for online-only participants.

All participants are required to complete a final project. The final project will be presented at finals, or part of one of our final events including our AI film festival and product lab. More details about that will be published later this month.

If you have any questions, find Gene and ask on Discord!

Looking forward to a great year.

```
1. Cognitive architecture

5. HTTP bridge for tool integration
```

Gene is teaching people to use the *surface* of agents. You've built the *infrastructure*.
